_pilot_titles: How sourdough baking changed my relationship with time
The unexpected joy of reading paper books
What I learned from my first marathon
Why your morning routine isn't working
notes: Judges frequently critique verbosity and repetition, emphasizing the need for more concise and direct prose. They also often point out the lack of novel or specific information, suggesting a preference for well-substantiated and concrete insights.

decisions: Consolidated 'lacks depth' to level 3 only; mild form was inconsistent with codebook v0.2 'observable severity' criterion
Struck 'fails to' from level 3 due to substring overlap with level 5 phrases ('fails to provide', 'fails to deliver')
Struck 'generally well-structured, but' as polarity-fragile per codebook §X (depends on subsequent clause)
Struck 'not particularly informative' as polarity-fragile (specific negation form, won't generalize)
Demoted 'redundant', 'extraneous', 'unsubstantive' from level 5 to level 3 — these are direct but not absolutist/contempt-laden per codebook v0.2 level-5 definition
Demoted 'could be more concise' from level 3 to level 2 — common hedged form, mild in register
Stripped leading whitespace from 'lean toward wordiness'
